projectDescription: IRB (Interactive Ruby) is Ruby's built-in REPL that lets you interactively evaluate Ruby expressions, debug programs with breakpoints, and extend the session with custom commands.
keyFeatures: Interactive Ruby REPL with syntax highlighting and auto-completion
binding.irb breakpoint for in-process debugging sessions
debug.gem integration for step-debugging, frame navigation, and breakpoints
Type-based completion with IRB::TypeCompletor for chained method suggestions
Extensible via custom commands (IRB::Command::Base) and helper methods (IRB::HelperMethod::Base)
Configurable via .irbrc file, IRB.conf hash, environment variables, and CLI options
Built-in commands: ls, show_source, show_doc, whereami, edit, measure, history
Workspace navigation with pushws/popws/cd for exploring object contexts
Input history with filtering, persistent across sessions
